summary_one_line: Take false side when a SQL column reference resolves to a real table column (cntTab!=0), requiring a valid table.column token that matches a schema name.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A SQL statement whose column expression names a table/column that survives lookupName's SrcList scan so cntTab becomes non-zero before line 97941 (e.g. a qualified reference like 'M.L' whose table prefix matches a SrcList table name via sqlite3StrICmp), making zDb==0 && zTab!=0 && cntTab==0 false.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed begins 'SELECT M.L' — a dotted table.column token. Per-role coverage shows the winner alone walks the zDb/table-matching block (97847-97861) and the rowid path at 98025, and at the blocker line 97941 it takes the false side (T=0 F=1 for W), i.e. cntTab incremented so the trigger-fallback guard is false.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry degenerate column tokens ('SELECT CC...', 'SELECT+SS...') that do not match any SrcList table, so cntTab stays 0; the loser exclusively reaches the double-quoted-string / error path at 98116-98162 and at line 97941 takes only the true side (T=0 F=20 for L) — the trigger/upsert fallback.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback: the sqlite3StrICmp calls comparing the input column/table token against schema names emit Hamming/matching-prefix distance buckets; an input whose token is closer to a real table/column name produces a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, letting value_profile climb toward a token that fully matches and increments cntTab, which naive (edge-only) cannot gradient toward.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the trigger/upsert fallback guard and its globally blocked side is the false branch.
Both decisive pairs are won by the value_profile-bearing fuzzer with delta value_profile.
The second decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over cmplog, again with delta value_profile.
The winner-resolving seed begins with a dotted table.column token 'SELECT M.L'.
Loser-blocking seeds carry degenerate column tokens beginning 'SELECT CC'.
At the blocker line the winner takes the false side while the loser takes only the true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L97941  T=0 F=1  T=20 F=0  if( zDb==0 && zTab!=0 && cntTab==0 ){  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winner alone exercises the table-matching block in lookupName, indicating its token resolves against a SrcList/schema name.
The loser exclusively reaches the no-match double-quoted-string / error fallback path.
value_profile feedback adds a CMP_MAP bucket when an input's CMP operands are closer to matching than any previously seen, preserving it as corpus.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP distance bucket encodes matching-prefix length for string compares such as sqlite3StrICmp.
naive has only per-edge hit-count feedback, with no CMP-distance gradient signal.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Not applicable: both decisive pairs carry delta value_profile (value_profile>naive and value_profile_cmplog>cmplog), so the attributed technique is the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient, not I2SRandReplace. Lineage dash-row verification is only mandated for I2SRandReplace claims, so no db_query.py lineage run was performed.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a column-name string compare (the gated equality) where value_profile fails to resolve the false side faster than naive, i.e. removing the CMP_MAP gradient produces no winner/loser separation at the cntTab guard.

weakest_evidence_point: The seed evidence is single-sided (one winner seed) and I infer the table-prefix match drives cntTab from source structure rather than from a direct trace of which sqlite3StrICmp comparison the winner's token satisfied.
